title: Doodia heterophylla
taxonomy: Doodia heterophylla is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Polypodiaceae, commonly known as the polypody fern family. It belongs to the genus Doodia, which consists of several species of ferns. The species Doodia heterophylla is native to Australia and New Guinea. It is also known as the variable-rachis fern due to its unique rachis characteristics. This fern species is often found growing in shady and humid environments.
uses: Doodia heterophylla, a plant native to Southeast Asia, has been traditionally used in medicine for its various health benefits. The plant's leaves and roots are used to treat fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions. Its leaves are also used to treat skin conditions such as eczema and dermatitis. Furthermore, Doodia heterophylla is believed to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help protect against cell damage and promote overall health. Additionally, the plant is used to treat digestive issues and as a natural remedy for various health ailments.

side_effects: Doodia heterophylla, also known as Ruffey, is a plant used in traditional medicine.
